codeReview.summary: The scaffold implementation is mostly complete (Drizzle, Vitest, scripts, and shadcn artifacts are present), but it violates a core requirement by using Next.js 16 instead of the required Next.js 15.

issues.description: Feature requirement explicitly calls for a Next.js 15 project, but dependency is pinned to `next: 16.2.3`. This is a direct mismatch with the feature description/expected behavior and can introduce incompatible runtime/API behavior relative to the mission contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission/library state assumes Next.js 15 while the scaffold command `create-next-app@latest` now resolves to Next.js 16, creating version drift unless explicitly pinned.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description in mission features.json requires Next.js 15; implemented commit sets `package.json` `next` to `16.2.3` (line 24). `.factory/library/architecture.md` also states the app is built on Next.js 15.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill's required TDD-first step is not a good fit for pure scaffolding features; worker had to deviate and document that deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript/handoff `skillFeedback.deviations` records skipping TDD for this infrastructure-only feature and suggests updating skill guidance.

summary: Reviewed feature `scaffold-nextjs-project` (commit `5ed2d9b`) against mission expectations and transcript evidence. Found one blocking correctness issue: the project is on Next.js 16, not the required Next.js 15. Report marked as fail.
